servers.description: Relative path for FFI/tool interaction (simulated server)

500.description: Failed to get scaled image info

200.description: Combined JSON schemas for V1 endpoints

post.summary:
Build the JSON response for `v1/static/info`. Uses a process-wide
cache so the hot path is a `Cow::Borrowed` over a static byte slice
— no serde work after the first call.

200.description: Static build info + capabilities + RIAPI schema

color_profiles.description: Allows use of color profiles other than sRGB. This is now widely supported.

modern_web_safe.description: Enables jpeg, gif, png, webp, color_profiles

web_safe.description:
Enables all formats that are 'safe' to display for all browsers.
Enables jpeg, gif, png.
WebP is at 97%, but is still excluded.

Build001.description:
Represents a complete build job, combining IO objects with a framewise operation graph.
TODO: cleanup builder_config.

Build001GraphRecording.description:

Contains flags that instruct how job execution is recorded during execution.

codec_priority_default.description:
Snake-case codec-priority preset this build defaults to
(`CODEC_PRIORITY_DEFAULT`).

compile_deny_decode.description:
Formats the build-ceiling permanently denies for decode
(`COMPILE_DENY_DECODE`). Snake-case format names.

compile_deny_encode.description:
Formats the build-ceiling permanently denies for encode
(`COMPILE_DENY_ENCODE`). Snake-case format names.

features.description: Feature flags active in this build (e.g. `"c-codecs"`, `"zen-codecs"`).

CapsSummary.description:
Union of the capability flags / ranges across every codec (for a
given role) that backs a format in the current build. Booleans are
OR-merged; ranges take the widest span.

Fields match the zencodec capability surface 1:1 where sensible, so
clients can reason about codec behaviour without calling into
codec-specific types.

animation.description:
Encode-only: whether the codec supports animation emission.
Decode-only: whether the codec supports animation parsing.

effort_range.description: `None` if the codec has no effort knob.

push_rows.description:
Encode-only: whether the codec supports row-level push encoding.
Decode-only: whether the codec supports row-level streaming decode.

quality_range.description: `None` if the codec is lossless-only / has no quality knob.

CodecAvailability.description: Per-codec row of the static info response.

CodecKillbits.description:
Codec-level killbits: allow/deny individual named encoders and
decoders.

Sits alongside `FormatKillbits` on `ExecutionSecurity`. Resolution
order:

1. Build-time ceiling (compile gates) picks which named codecs exist.
2. Trusted policy may `allow_*` (positive list — this is the only way
   to *allow* a compiled codec that was absent from the ambient grid,
   though today any compiled-in codec is available by default) or
   `deny_*`.
3. Job-level requests may only `deny_*` — no `allow_*`, same rule as
   `FormatKillbits`.

When the grid denies every encoder for a format, the format itself
flips to "encode: false" in `v1/context/get_net_support`, with
`reasons: ["no_available_encoder"]` on the format entry.

allow_decoders.description:
Allow only these decoders; all other decoders are denied.
Mutually exclusive with `deny_decoders`. Trusted-policy-only.

allow_encoders.description:
Allow only these encoders; all other encoders are denied.
Mutually exclusive with `deny_encoders`. Trusted-policy-only.

deny_decoders.description:
Deny these decoders. Mutually exclusive with `allow_decoders`.
Allowed at any layer.

deny_encoders.description:
Deny these encoders. Mutually exclusive with `allow_encoders`.
Allowed at any layer.

CodecRole.description:
Role a codec plays. Mirrors `NamedEncoderName` / `NamedDecoderName`
as a single axis.

CodecSubstitutionAnnotation.description:
Annotation attached to a per-encode-step response when the dispatcher
served a specific-codec `EncoderPreset` by routing to a different
codec for the same wire format.

Surfaces on [`crate::EncodeResult::annotations`] — one per encoded
image — and never represents an error. The output bytes are valid for
the advertised format; this annotation only tells the caller *which*
backend produced them and why the requested one was skipped.

actual.description:
Wire name of the codec that actually produced output
(e.g. `zen_jpeg_encoder`).

codec_priority.description:
Build-time codec-priority flavor that selected the substitute
order. `"v3_zen_first"` on upstream / V3 forks,
`"v2_classic_first"` on V2 forks. The field is informational —
callers can log it to distinguish an unexpected pick driven by
priority from one driven by killbits.

dropped_fields.description:
Field values from the request that were dropped because the
substitute codec doesn't support them
(e.g. `"preset.zlib_compression"` on the lodepng fallback path).

field_translations.description:
Human-readable translation notes, one per field the preset carried
that was remapped onto the substitute codec's configuration
(e.g. `"preset.quality → zen.quality"`).

reason.description: Why the dispatcher skipped the requested codec.

requested.description:
Wire name of the codec the `EncoderPreset` named
(e.g. `mozjpeg_encoder`).

Color.description: Represents arbitrary colors (not color space specific)

oneOf.description: Hex in RRGGBBAA (css) form or variant thereof

hex.description: Hex in RRGGBBAA (css) form or variant thereof

oneOf.description:
Forward-extensible annotation bag. Mirror of
[`EncodeResult::annotations`] for the decode side.

DecoderSubstitutionAnnotation.description:
Mirror of [`CodecSubstitutionAnnotation`] for the decode side.

Surfaces on [`crate::DecodeResult::annotations`] when a decoder
substitution happened (e.g. `zen_jpeg_decoder` was denied, so the
dispatcher used `mozjpeg_decoder` instead). Shape mirrors the encoder
annotation for UI consistency.

Edge.description: Operation nodes are connected by edges. JSON only.

EdgeKind.description:
Operation nodes are connected by edges. Many operations require both an input and a canvas node.

In the future, some operations may have multiple inputs, and new edge types may be introduced for non-bitmap data.

EncodeAnnotations.description:
Bag of forward-extensible annotations attached to a single encoded
image in [`crate::EncodeResult`].

Each field is optional; new annotation kinds can be added without
breaking callers that deserialize older messages. Callers should
treat unknown fields as ignorable.

oneOf.description:
Set iff the dispatcher substituted the requested codec with a
different one that produces the same wire format.

Forward-extensible annotation bag. Populated when the dispatcher
substituted the requested codec, surfaced unit-warning output,
or otherwise has non-error information about how this particular
encode step was served. Omitted from the wire when empty.

Requires a quality profile to be specified
Specify "allow" to enable specific formats (like webp, avif, jxl) and features
(like jpeg_progressive, jpeg_xyb, color_profiles), (or sets thereof, like web_safe and modern_web_safe).

auto.description:
Requires a quality profile to be specified
Specify "allow" to enable specific formats (like webp, avif, jxl) and features
(like jpeg_progressive, jpeg_xyb, color_profiles), (or sets thereof, like web_safe and modern_web_safe).

oneOf.description: Whether to use, disable, or keep lossless encoding.
Applies a matte - background color to the image to eliminate transparency.

quality_profile.description: A quality profile to use. Will provide default 'allowed' formats, but 'allowed' takes priority.

quality_profile_dpr.description: Adjusts the quality profile, assuming a 150ppi display and 3x CSS pixel ratio. 3 is the default. lower values will increase quality, higher values will decrease quality. Useful when not using srcset/picture, just img src. Ex.

oneOf.description:
Requires a file format to be specified, and allows for specific encoder hints.
Specific format features can be specified in 'allow', such as jxl_animation, avif_animation, etc.

format.description:
Requires a file format to be specified, and allows for specific encoder hints.
Specific format features can be specified in 'allow', such as jxl_animation, avif_animation, etc.

oneOf.description: Whether to use, disable, or keep lossless encoding.
Applies a matte - background color to the image to eliminate transparency.

quality_profile_dpr.description: Adjusts the quality profile, assuming a 150ppi display and 3x CSS pixel ratio. 3 is the default. lower values will increase quality, higher values will decrease quality. Useful when not using srcset/picture, just img src. Ex.

EncoderPreset.description: Encoder presets (each with optional configuration). These are exposed by the JSON API.

oneOf.description:
Per-codec decode/encode killbits. Complements `formats` by
letting operators allow/deny individual named backends within a
format (e.g. forbid mozjpeg while keeping zen_jpeg_encoder). See
`CodecKillbits` for the full description.

Same layering rules as `formats`:
- Trusted policy may use any shape (`allow_*`, `deny_*`).
- Job-level `security` may only `deny_*`.

Absent = no change at this layer.

Boxed to keep `ExecutionSecurity` small — `CodecKillbits` carries
~160 bytes of inline list capacity when fully populated.

Per-format decode/encode killbits. Part of the three-layer codec
gating system:

- Trusted policy (via `v1/context/set_policy`) may use any form
  (`allow_decode`, `deny_decode`, `allow_encode`, `deny_encode`, or
  `formats` table).
- Job-level `security` on `Build001`/`Execute001` may only *deny*
  (no `allow_*`, no table entries with `decode: true`/`encode: true`).

Boxed to keep `ExecutionSecurity` small on the stack — killbits carry
~120 bytes of inline capacity for the per-format lists and table.

Absent = no change at this layer.

max_input_file_bytes.description:
Maximum bytes for a single codec input stream (e.g., WebP buffering).
Default: 256 MB. Set to `None` to disable.

max_json_bytes.description:
Maximum bytes for a JSON API payload before deserialization.
Default: 64 MB. Set to `None` to disable.

max_total_file_pixels.description:
Maximum total decoded pixels across all frames in a file.
For animation, this is checked as frame_count * width * height.
Default: 400 megapixels. Set to `None` to disable.

FormatAvailability.description:
Per-format availability + metadata + capability union across this
build's enabled encoders and decoders.

oneOf.description:
Union of all enabled-decoder capabilities for this format.
`None` when no decoder backs the format.

Union of all enabled-encoder capabilities for this format in
this build. `None` when no encoder backs the format.

FormatKillbits.description:
Killbits for decode/encode on a per-format basis.

Three mutually exclusive request shapes:

- `allow_decode` / `allow_encode`: the listed formats are the *only* ones
  permitted. Anything else is denied. (Trusted-policy layer only — rejected
  at job level.)
- `deny_decode` / `deny_encode`: the listed formats are denied. Everything
  else carries over from the layer above.
- `formats`: an explicit table of `{format: {decode, encode}}` pairs.
  (Trusted-policy layer only — job level may set fields to `false` only.)

Validation at deserialize time rejects mixing forms (e.g., `allow_decode`
together with `deny_decode`, or a list form together with `formats`).

allow_decode.description:
Allow only these for decode (rejects any format not listed).
Mutually exclusive with `deny_decode` and with `formats`.

allow_encode.description:
Allow only these for encode. Mutually exclusive with `deny_encode` and
with `formats`.

deny_decode.description: Deny these for decode. Mutually exclusive with `allow_decode`.

deny_encode.description: Deny these for encode. Mutually exclusive with `allow_encode`.

formats.description: Per-format table form. Mutually exclusive with the list forms above.

propertyNames.description:
The set of image formats that can be referenced by killbits.

`#[non_exhaustive]` so new formats can be added without semver breakage to
downstream matches. Serialized as `snake_case` strings.

FormatPermissions.description:
Per-format decode/encode permissions.

Used inside `FormatKillbits::formats` to express a full allow/deny grid in
one request body.

decode_reasons.description:
Machine-readable reasons a cell ended up denied. Present on
formats whose decode/encode was turned off by the codec-level
fold (e.g. every mapped encoder killed) or explicit codec
killbits.

Framewise.description:
Represents an operation graph or series (series is simpler to think about and suitable for most tasks).
Operation graphs *may* be applied to each frame in the source data - thus 'Framewise'.

Graph.description: An operation graph; should be directed and acyclic. JSON only.

ImageFormat.description:
The set of image formats that can be referenced by killbits.

`#[non_exhaustive]` so new formats can be added without semver breakage to
downstream matches. Serialized as `snake_case` strings.

IoDirection.description: We must mark IO objects as data sources or data destinations.

oneOf.description:
# Security
This path is used as-is with no sanitization. The caller is responsible
for validating paths before constructing this variant. Server deployments
must reject absolute paths, `..` traversal, and symlinks at the API layer.

file.description:
# Security
This path is used as-is with no sanitization. The caller is responsible
for validating paths before constructing this variant. Server deployments
must reject absolute paths, `..` traversal, and symlinks at the API layer.

IoEnum.description: Describes (or contains) a data source or destination

IoObject.description: Data source or destination (including IO ID).

JobOptions.description:
Reserved for future options controlling backends, color management,
and other job-level behavior. Currently empty.

NamedDecoderName.description:
Named decoders that can be individually allowed/denied via the
codec-level killbits grid. See `NamedEncoderName` for the design
rationale (nominal enum, feature gating handled at the core layer).

NamedEncoderName.description:
Named encoders that can be individually allowed/denied via the
codec-level killbits grid.

These are the wire-side names of the specific `NamedEncoders` variants
implemented by `imageflow_core`. This type intentionally lists every
known encoder — feature gating is handled at the core layer, so the
enum stays "purely nominal" on the types side. An encoder being
listed here doesn't mean the current build provides it; that question
is answered by the runtime net_support grid.

`#[non_exhaustive]` so new backends can be added without semver breakage.

codecs.description: Per-codec availability, keyed by snake_case codec name.

formats.description:
Per-format decode/encode availability under the combined grid
(layer 1 ∩ layer 2 ∩ layer 3, with the codec-availability fold-in).

Node.description:
Represents a image operation. Currently used both externally (for JSON API) and internally.
The most important data type

PixelFormat.description:
Memory layout for pixels.
sRGB w/ gamma encoding assumed for 8-bit channels.

RiapiCategory.description:
Coarse grouping used to drive the server-side cache-key inclusion
rules (see [`ServerRecommendations`]).

accept_header_origin.description:
For `accept.*` keys, the HTTP `Accept` header media type that the
server should translate into this knob at the edge.

cache_relevant.description:
Whether the key participates in the cached representation the
server should vary on. `false` for trace/debug knobs.

RiapiSchema.description: RIAPI schema bundle for this build.

ignores_unknown_keys.description:
Whether the parser silently drops unknown keys. Useful for clients
that want to probe behaviour.

RiapiValueKind.description:
Kind of value a RIAPI key accepts. Present to help clients offer
inline validation.

ScalingFloatspace.description: The color space to blend/combine pixels in. Downscaling is best done in linear light.

ServerRecommendations.description:
Opinionated deployment recommendations. These are suggestions only —
the server chooses how to apply them.

accept_header_translation.description:
Map of `Accept` header media type → RIAPI key expression that the
edge should synthesize. Example: `"image/webp"` → `"accept.webp=1"`.

include_in_cache_key_prefix.description:
Key patterns the edge should include in the cache key prefix
(wildcards allowed, e.g. `"accept.*"`).

notes.description:
Freeform guidance clarifying subtle points (e.g. why we prefer
`accept.*` in the cache key to `Vary: Accept`).

strip_from_cache_key.description: RIAPI keys the edge should strip before hashing the cache key.

SetPolicyRequest.description:
Request body for `v1/context/set_policy`. Sets the trusted policy
layer (layer 2 of the killbits system). Idempotent once set;
calling again without `require_unlocked` is accepted only if the
new policy narrows (never widens) the existing one.

require_unlocked.description: If true, error when a trusted policy is already set. Default: false.

SubstitutionReason.description:
Reason a specific-codec `EncoderPreset` was served by a different
codec than the one the caller named.

Appears in [`CodecSubstitutionAnnotation::reason`]. The enum is
`#[non_exhaustive]` so new reasons can be added without a semver
break. Serialized as `snake_case` strings.
